description_long: This is a large, weathered wooden crate with a robust, reinforced frame and horizontal planks making up its sides. The wood is stained and marked, showing signs of age and use, with visible nail heads and some surface discoloration. The top of the crate features a bold, circular black stamp with a logo and text, suggesting it is used for shipping or storage, possibly for industrial or commercial purposes. One side of the crate displays a torn Priority Mail shipping label, indicating it has been used for postal or freight transport. The crate appears solid and heavy, with no visible handles or locking mechanisms, and the lid is flush with the top frame. Its rugged construction and utilitarian design make it suitable for placement in urban environments such as warehouses, garages, or loading docks.
description: A large, weathered wooden shipping crate with a stamped logo on top and a torn shipping label on one side. The crate is reinforced, solid, and shows signs of heavy use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crate's wooden planks, reinforced frame, and a clear view of the circular logo stamped on the top.
description_view_1: Side view with similar wood texture and logo partially visible, but no shipping label present.
description_view_2: Another side view, nearly identical to view 1, with wood grain and logo visible but no label.
description_view_3: Rear view featuring a torn Priority Mail shipping label affixed to the wooden planks, with the logo still visible on the top.
